polymorphic-collections.label: Polymorphic Collections
polymorphic-collections.summary: Loop over different object types and call the same method name on each object.

try_polymorphic_collections_sketch0.prompt: Create a base class `Shape` with a method `area()`. Implement subclasses `Circle` and `Square` that override `area()` to return the correct area. Use a list to store instances of these shapes and print their areas.

sc-polymorphic-collections-0.title: Understanding Polymorphism
sc-polymorphic-collections-0.prompt: What is the main advantage of using polymorphism in object-oriented programming?
sc-polymorphic-collections-0.hint: Think about how polymorphism affects code flexibility and maintenance.

help.concept: Polymorphism allows objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass.
help.hint_1: Polymorphism enables methods to be called on objects without knowing their exact class.
help.hint_2: It allows for code that is more flexible and easier to maintain.

options.a: It allows for code reuse through inheritance.
options.b: It enables objects to be treated as instances of their superclass.
options.c: It provides a way to hide implementation details.

sc-polymorphic-collections-1.title: Polymorphic Method Calls
sc-polymorphic-collections-1.prompt: Which of the following best describes a polymorphic method call?
sc-polymorphic-collections-1.hint: Consider how polymorphic method calls work with different object types.

help.concept: A polymorphic method call is when a method is called on an object without knowing its exact class.
help.hint_1: Polymorphic method calls allow the same method name to be used on different objects.
help.hint_2: The method that gets executed depends on the object's actual class.

options.a: A method call that changes the object's class.
options.b: A method call that is the same for all objects of a class.
options.c: A method call that can execute different methods depending on the object's class.
options.d: A method call that requires the object's exact class to be known.

fb-polymorphic-collections-0.title: Base Class Definition
fb-polymorphic-collections-0.prompt: In a polymorphic collection, all objects must share a common [blank1].
fb-polymorphic-collections-0.hint: Think about what allows different objects to be treated the same way.

help.concept: A common the missing term or interface allows different objects to be treated uniformly.
help.hint_1: The commonality is usually defined by a shared superclass or interface.
help.hint_2: This shared element allows for polymorphic behavior.

fb-polymorphic-collections-0.template: In a polymorphic collection, all objects must share a common [blank1].

fb-polymorphic-collections-1.title: Method Overriding
fb-polymorphic-collections-1.prompt: In polymorphism, subclasses override methods to provide specific [blank1].
fb-polymorphic-collections-1.hint: Consider what subclasses provide when they override methods.

help.concept: Method overriding allows subclasses to provide specific the missing term for inherited methods.
help.hint_1: Overriding methods allows subclasses to define their own behavior.
help.hint_2: This behavior is specific to the subclass and differs from the base class.

fb-polymorphic-collections-1.template: In polymorphism, subclasses override methods to provide specific [blank1].

mc-polymorphic-collections-0.title: Benefits of Polymorphism
mc-polymorphic-collections-0.prompt: Select all the benefits of using polymorphism in programming.
mc-polymorphic-collections-0.hint: Think about how polymorphism affects code design and flexibility.

help.concept: Polymorphism enhances code flexibility, reusability, and maintainability.
help.hint_1: Polymorphism allows for flexible code that can handle new object types easily.
help.hint_2: It also promotes code reusability and easier maintenance.

dr-polymorphic-collections-0.title: Order of Method Calls
dr-polymorphic-collections-0.prompt: Arrange the steps in the correct order for calling a polymorphic method on a collection of objects.
dr-polymorphic-collections-0.hint: Consider the sequence from defining the method to calling it on each object.

help.concept: The process involves defining a method in a base class, overriding it in subclasses, and then calling it on each object in a collection.
help.hint_1: Start with defining the method in the base class.
help.hint_2: Next, override the method in each subclass before calling it on the objects.
